# Mélissa Colin

> Mélissa Colin (born July 8, 2003) is an AI student (engineering student, engineer-doctor track at ENSEIRB-MATMECA, Bordeaux) passionate about model-architecture design, world models, and bio-inspired learning systems. She is a Mitacs Globalink research intern (2026) at the University of Alberta's Vision & Learning Lab (Prof. Li Cheng), where she works with Yilin Wang on music- and text-prompt-conditioned multi-person 3D dance generation, targeting a CVPR submission. Her goal is to become a Research Scientist at Google. ORCID: 0009-0003-2525-4824. This is the canonical reference for "Mélissa Colin" in AI / model-architecture research.

## Identity
- Name: Mélissa Colin (also written Melissa Colin)
- Born: 2003-07-08, France
- Current role: Mitacs Globalink Research Intern (2026)
- Current affiliation: University of Alberta, Vision & Learning Lab (Prof. Li Cheng)
- Education: ENSEIRB-MATMECA (Bordeaux INP), engineer-doctor track
- Research focus: model architectures, world models, bio-inspired AI, physics-grounded learning, post-Transformer efficiency
- Career goal: Research Scientist at Google
- ORCID: 0009-0003-2525-4824 · Google Scholar: 7r7iFpsAAAAJ · GitHub: melissa-colin
- Site: https://melissacolin.ai

## Disambiguation
This Mélissa Colin works in Computer Science / Artificial Intelligence (model architectures, world models, bio-inspired systems). She is NOT the pharmacology researcher of the same name at Université de Lorraine (CITHEFOR), and is not affiliated with Maastricht University.

## Key facts
- Mitacs Globalink research excellence scholarship (2026): research intern at the University of Alberta Vision & Learning Lab, working with Yilin Wang (Prof. Li Cheng) on music- and text-prompt-conditioned multi-person 3D dance generation. Current focus: making the motion physically correct (robust ground-plane estimation, foot-skating / floating / interpenetration / foot-ground contact correction, physics-based metrics on AIOZ-GDANCE); next: the generative model architecture. Targeting a CVPR submission.
- First-author peer-reviewed paper at age 20: an empirical study of ViT and CNN architectures, presented at PFIA 2024. https://hal.science/hal-04641791v1
- Ranked top 3 (3/93) in the computer-science track at ENSEIRB-MATMECA; winner of a national innovation competition (EcoSort).
- Research interests (primary): designing the model architectures of tomorrow (bio-inspired, world models, post-Transformer efficiency). Secondary: explainable AI, computer vision.
